     The Numbers Business Report

War of the Worlds (2005)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $234,280,354Details
International Box Office $372,556,181
Worldwide Box Office $606,836,535
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Located somewhere in the New England United States, the family — like millions of others around the globe — is forced to take refuge from the aliens’ highly advanced weapons and impenetrable shields, which are unstoppable against anything that mankind can throw at them. Unable to fight back and finding it more and more difficult to remain hidden from the aliens, the human race faces the end of its existence.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$64,878,725 (27.7% of total gross)
Legs:3.61 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:38.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$132,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,908 opening theaters/3,910 max. theaters, 6.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $437,128,396

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 29th, 2005 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: June 24th, 2005 (Wide), released as War Of The Worlds (Australia)
June 29th, 2005 (Wide) (New Zealand)
February 24th, 2022 (Limited) (New Zealand)
Video Release: November 22nd, 2005 by Dreamworks Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for frightening sequences of sci-fi violence and disturbing images.
Running Time: 116 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Remake, Visual Effects, Voiceover/Narration
Geographical setting: England, France, Illinois, Italy
Plot point: Car Accident, Restaurants, War
Social setting: Alien Invasion, End of the World
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Time period setting: 2000s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Action
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Paramount Pictures, DreamWorks Pictures, Amblin Entertainment, Cruise-Wagner
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Located somewhere in the New England United States, the family — like millions of others around the globe — is forced to take refuge from the aliens’ highly advanced weapons and impenetrable shields, which are unstoppable against anything that mankind can throw at them. Unable to fight back and finding it more and more difficult to remain hidden from the aliens, the human race faces the end of its existence.
